title: Taking of Kuat

The '''Taking of Kuat''' was a battle fought between [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] and [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] forces during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] for control of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]] and its vital [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]], owned by the Imperial-aligned company [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]], in [[8 ABY/Legends|8.5 ABY]]. The Imperial forces at Kuat—led by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Teren Rogriss]] and consisting of the [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] [[Aurora (Star Dreadnought)|''Aurora'']], three [[Battlecruiser/Legends|battlecruisers]], and over a dozen [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]]—faced only the Republic [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptors]] of [[Surprise Squadron]].

However, the [[Ion torpedo/Legends|ion weaponry]] of the A-wings disabled the systems of four [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]], while a [[Coded eraser virus|virus]] caused five Imperial [[Warship/Legends|warships]] to turn their [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] on allies. That turn of events, plus the fact that the New Republic owned a significant portion of the KDY shares and had [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruisers]] stationed near the [[Kuat system/Legends|Kuat system]], prompted the leading members of KDY to surrender to the New Republic forces, under the command of military figures Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]], and [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]], as well as [[Minister of Commerce]] [[Ral'Rai Muvunc]].

==Prelude==
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a conflict between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the forces of the New Republic were victorious in the [[Hunt for Zsinj|campaign]] against [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]], who ruled a significant expanse of [[Zsinj's Empire|territory]]. [[Post–Zsinj campaigns|Following]] this success, in [[8 ABY/Legends|8.5 ABY]] New Republic forces prepared to seize the vital [[Shipyard/Legends|shipbuilding facilities]] of [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] located at the Imperial world [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]] after the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], the New Republic's predecessor,<ref name="TEGTW" /> failed at a [[Battle for the Eclipse|previous attempt]] [[4 ABY/Legends|four years previously]].<ref name="Corrupt">''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]''</ref> The [[Kuati Sector Forces]] protecting Kuat contained the [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] [[Aurora (Star Dreadnought)|''Aurora'']], three [[Battlecruiser/Legends|battlecruisers]]—the ''[[Stellar Halo]]'', [[Luminous (battlecruiser)|''Luminous'']], and [[Event Horizon (battlecruiser)|''Event Horizon'']]—along with more than a dozen [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]], and was under the command of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Teren Rogriss]], sent from the Imperial world of [[Orinda/Legends|Orinda]] to helm the ''Aurora''.<ref name="TEGTW" />

[[File:TychoAtlas.jpg|thumb|left|100px|Tycho Celchu, leader of Surprise Squadron]]
Prominent New Republic military figures [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]], along with [[Minister of Commerce]] [[Ral'Rai Muvunc]] and [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]], created the plan to take Kuat<ref name="TEGTW" /> by exploiting certain holes in the KDY security, while Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] commanded the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] that contained the units that the New Republic would use to capture Kuat.<ref name="TEA" /> In the eight [[Standard month/Legends|months]] preceding the battle, a [[Unidentified holding company|holding company]] of which Muvunc was the chief executive acquired thirty-four percent of KDY shares from various investment companies and private trusts who had purchased the shares, almost valueless since the Empire had gained complete control of KDY's affairs, from various KDY investors. The New Republic assembled a large fleet of [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] at the [[Horthav system/Legends|Horthav system]], very close to Kuat.<ref name="TEGTW" />

Cracken, who was also the [[Director of New Republic Intelligence|Director]] of the [[New Republic Intelligence Service]], had [[Slicer/Legends|slicers]] under his guidance work for months to insert [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droids]] into the KDY [[Droid/Legends|droid]] pool and onto five key Kuati [[Warship/Legends|warships]], including the ''Event Horizon''. The droids loaded a [[Coded eraser virus|coded virus]] that erased all instances of its malignant programming onto the systems of all five warships. After the [[Computer virus/Legends|virus]] was finished uploading, a transmission was sent from the ''Event Horizon'' to the [[Venir system/Legends|Venir system]], where [[Surprise Squadron]], a [[Squadron/Legends|group]] of [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptors]] equipped with experimental [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion torpedoes]] and commanded by Celchu, waited.<ref name="TEGTW" />

==The battle==
Led by Celchu, Surprise Squadron [[Hyperspace/Legends|jumped]] to the [[Kuat system/Legends|Kuat system]], engaging the Kuati Sector Forces in the first action of the Taking of Kuat. Moments later, the crews of the ''Luminous'' and three other Star Destroyers struggled to get the warships' systems back after they were knocked out by the ion torpedoes of the A-wings. Due to the virus installed in their systems, the ''Event Horizon'' and the other four [[Star Destroyer]]s locked down their [[Hangar/Legends|hangars]] and turned their [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] on the other Kuati warships. In the midst of the battle, Muvunc held an emergency shareholder meeting with the directors of KDY, where Muvunc brought to light the fact that he owned the company that controlled thirty-four percent of the Kuati shares, therefore also controlling thirty-four percent of the company.<ref name="TEGTW" />

After some members pointed out that control of KDY was a military affair, Muvunc managed to convince three members of the board to vote their shares, which aggregated eighteen percent, in favor of surrendering to the New Republic after he informed the directors that his allies had taken out nine warships in a matter of [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]], and that the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]] could be in the Kuati [[Star system/Legends|system]] in moments. With the majority of KDY wanting to surrender, excluding KDY [[Chairman/Legends|Chairman]] [[Kateel of Kuhlvult]], the New Republic gained a victory without losing a single [[Pilot/Legends|pilot's]] life. Admiral Rogriss and his command crew were permitted to safely withdraw back to Orinda<ref name="TEGTW" /> after Rogriss ordered a retreat.<ref name="New" />

==Aftermath==
[[File:KuatDriveYards_EGTW.jpg|thumb|right|230px|The New Republic gained control of Kuat's orbital shipyards.]]
With Kuat and its subsidiary systems in their hands, the New Republic controlled a third of the settled [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] and had at its disposal the unparalleled shipbuilding docks of KDY, which were vital to any war effort.<ref name="New" /> However, Imperial forces would not give up so easily. Only [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] after Kuat's surrender, Imperial undercover [[Agent/Legends|agents]] [[Undercover attack on Kuat|attacked]] Kuat, activating the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrives]] of the ''Aurora'' and ''Stellar Halo'' and sending them into [[Kuat (star)|Kuat's sun]], as well as abducting KDY's key designers and fleeing to the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] aboard the half-complete [[Eclipse-class dreadnought|''Eclipse''-class dreadnought]] [[Eclipse (Eclipse-class)|''Eclipse'']]. The Imperial agents also triggered explosives that heavily damaged Kuat's shipyards,<ref name="TEGTW" /> so much so that construction on new [[Starship/Legends|ships]] was delayed indefinitely.<ref name="New" /> However, despite the damage and the loss of senior KDY designers, the New Republic was soon pumping out warships to add to Ackbar's fleet via the Kuat shipyards.<ref name="TEA" /> As a result of the victory, Ackbar worked on fortifying the New Republic's recent gain, while Warlord [[Treuten Teradoc]], a former Imperial, fell back to his own territory<ref name="New" /> of the [[Greater Maldrood]]<ref name="TEA" /> instead of continuing his hit-and-run attacks on the New Republic.<ref name="New" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Here's a story I'd always wanted to tell, and figured I'd never get the chance—the downfall of one of the Empire's major fortress worlds. I was amazed when Warfare rolled around, nobody had tackled it yet, and I realized I'd get my wish. And then I was stressed out, because I didn't want a big fleet action or starfighter heroics—I wanted something nobody would see coming.|Author Jason Fry, on writing the specifics of the battle|<ref name="Endnotes">{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/25851092826/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-13|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 13}}</ref>}}
The Taking of Kuat was first mentioned in [[2000]]'s [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']], written by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Kevin J. Anderson]], which outlined the basics of the battle only.<ref name="Time">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref> It also received a mention in [[2005]]'s ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', written by the same authors who wrote the original chronology,<ref name="New" /> and [[2009]]'s ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', written by Wallace and [[Jason Fry]].<ref name="TEA" /> Specifics of the Taking of Kuat were not released until [[2012]], when Fry's and [[Paul R. Urquhart]]'s book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' was released with a detailed description of the battle.<ref name="TEGTW" /> Fry later wrote in his [http://jasonfry.tumblr.com/post/20843004826/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-1 endnotes] to ''Warfare'' that he had always wanted to tell the story of the Taking of Kuat, getting his chance to do so finally in ''Warfare''. He wanted something that nobody would see coming as a major part of the battle, instead of a skirmish between rival fleets; Fry wrote Muvunc's part in the battle's result as an homage to [[Wikipedia:The Face (Vance)|''The Face'']], a book written by [[Wikipedia:Jack Vance|Jack Vance]].<ref name="Endnotes" />

A few inconsistencies exist between the information portrayed in ''The Essential Chronology'', ''The Essential Atlas'', and ''The Essential Guide to Warfare''.<ref name="Time" /><ref name="TEA" /><ref name="TEGTW" /> The ''Chronology'' infers that a [[Space warfare/Legends|fleet battle]] took place, damaging the stardocks, and Rogriss retreated after being [[Blood/Legends|bloodied]] in the battle.<ref name="Time" /> This is not supported in ''Warfare'', which states that no fleet battle took place and that Rogriss left for Orinda before the Imperial undercover counterattack damaged the shipyards.<ref name="TEGTW" /> The ''Chronology'' and the ''Atlas'' state that the lead KDY designers escaped Kuat of their own will,<ref name="Time" /> with the ''Atlas'' also adding that the staff fled during the battle,<ref name="TEA" /> while ''Warfare'' has them abducted by the Imperial agents during the attack afterward.<ref name="TEGTW" /> On all of these points, this article assumes that ''Warfare'' is correct, as it is the most recent source published.
